content: RAG chunking pitfalls: Too-small chunks lack meaning and miss retrieval; too-large chunks introduce noise and suffer lost-in-the-middle problem; fixed-size splitting destroys document structure especially tables and headers; semantic chunking is computationally expensive; overlap without structure awareness still loses boundary context; PDFs need specialized parsers to preserve tables/headers before chunking; contextual retrieval (LLM-generated chunk descriptions) is expensive but improves quality; fundamental rule is chunks must make sense without surrounding context.
